title: Bitter Fish Pickle Halal Comfort
description: A silky and bitter Algerian-inspired dish featuring halal-friendly fish marinated in a blend of sour pickles, herbs, and spices for sick-day comfort.

ingredients.name: Fish fillets
ingredients.notes: Choose a mild-flavored fish such as tilapia or cod.

ingredients.name: Pickled vegetables (cucumbers, carrots)
ingredients.notes: Ensure they are halal-certified if you're concerned about dietary restrictions.

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: For added flavor and health benefits.

ingredients.name: Onion, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: To enhance the dish's depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Red pepper flakes (optional)
ingredients.notes: For a touch of heat, but optional as the pickles are already sour.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: To sauté and marinate ingredients together.

ingredients.name: Rice vinegar or lemon juice
ingredients.notes: For the marinade to keep fish tender and flavorful.

instructions: Wash hands thoroughly. Preheat oven to 200°C (392°F).
Prepare a shallow baking dish by lining it with parchment paper or aluminum foil.
Place fish fillets in the prepared baking dish, skin side up if applicable.
Mix together garlic, chopped onion, dill, red pepper flakes (if using), and rice vinegar or lemon juice. Spread evenly over the fish fillets for even marination.
Drizzle olive oil over the mixture to coat thoroughly. Ensure all sides of the fish are covered with marinade.
Bake in preheated oven for 15 minutes, then remove from oven. Allow to cool slightly and serve as a snack or side dish.
Optional: Serve alongside additional pickles or vegetables for extra crunch.
